label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 6

extraction.one_line_summary: KUSS Sepp won from 40-rider breakaway on Javalambre climb, MARTINEZ Lenny took red jersey becoming youngest grand tour leader since 1904, EVENEPOEL lost 4+ minutes
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma planted three riders (Kuss, Van Baal, Teunissen) in 40-man break, overwhelming numerical advantage.
EVENEPOEL Remco deliberately surrendered jersey rather than defend, signaling intentional tactical concession at 85km to summit.
ROGLIC and VINGEGAARD chased together from peloton, cementing co-leadership dynamic rather than attacking each other on Javalambre.
MARTINEZ Lenny only 17 seconds down pre-stage, positioned on BARDET wheel specifically targeting GC takeover from breakaway.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Won while sacrificing 26 seconds to Martinez, showing Jumbo prioritized stage win over minimizing GC gaps
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: positive: Youngest grand tour leader since 1904, rode aggressively against Bardet suggesting unwillingness to cooperate with experienced climbers
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Lost 4+ minutes and dropped to 9th GC, race essentially over unless later collapse by others
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: Crashed out during frantic break formation, suggesting illness compromised bike handling in early positioning battles
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Chased with Vingegaard but didn't attack him, confirming Jumbo won't fracture leadership until later stages
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: Friction with Martinez over cooperation suggests younger riders no longer deferring to veterans in breakaways
